description_long: This object consists of a square, orange terracotta-style plant pot filled with dark soil, from which several brown-capped mushrooms with pale stems are growing. The mushrooms vary in size, with some larger and more mature, and others smaller and more slender, clustered together in the soil. The pot has straight, slightly flared sides and a smooth, unadorned surface, giving it a modern and minimalistic appearance. The soil appears slightly uneven and textured, providing a natural base for the mushrooms. The mushrooms have rounded, dome-shaped caps with a matte finish and smooth surfaces. There are no visible decorative elements or plant labels attached to the pot or the mushrooms.
description: A plain orange terracotta-style pot filled with dark soil and several brown-capped mushrooms with pale stems growing from it.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms of varying sizes are visible, with the largest cap on the right and a smaller one on the left, all emerging from the dark soil in the orange pot.
description_view_1: The view shows the mushrooms from a different angle, highlighting the curvature of their stems and the close grouping of the caps, with the largest mushrooms more centered.
description_view_2: This side emphasizes the overlapping mushroom caps, with the largest cap in the foreground and a smaller mushroom behind it, all within the orange pot.
description_view_3: The mushrooms are seen from the opposite side, with the largest cap now on the left and a clear view of the smallest mushroom in the back right corner of the pot.
